target.name: Mesh Optical Technologies
target.founder: Travis Brashears, Cameron Ramos, Serena Grown-Haeberli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Mesh Optical Technologies is a Series A semiconductor company that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms, including same-stage comparables Substrate ($1.0B valuation, Series A) and Rain AI ($500M, Series A), as well as adjacent-stage peers Etched ($5.0B, Series B) and Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $50M raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies $100M. Peer-median analysis across the four sampled comparables yields $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied due to the absence of revenue disclosure, which is standard for early-stage private firms. The semiconductor sector median P/S multiple across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD stands at 20.3x, but lacks a target numerator.

The triangulated range across the two applicable methods is $85M (low), $550M (median), and $1.1B (high). The wide dispersion reflects tension between stage-based progression and peer-market comparables. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, the maturity and defensibility of the optical chip architecture, and whether comparables like Substrate and Rain AI represent true market-clearing valuations or represent outliers driven by sector momentum.
